questions.text: Which is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile River is approximately 6,650 km long.

questions.text: The Sahara is the largest desert in the world.
questions.explanation: The Sahara Desert in Africa is the largest hot desert.

questions.text: Mount Everest is located in which mountain range?
questions.explanation: Mount Everest is in the Himalayas on the Nepal-Tibet border.

questions.text: Match countries with their capitals:
questions.explanation: These are the official capital cities of these nations.

questions.text: Which of these are island nations? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Iceland, Japan, Philippines, and New Zealand are island nations. Spain is mostly peninsular.

questions.text: Assertion: The Equator is the longest latitude line. Reason: It divides Earth into Northern and Southern hemispheres.
options: A. Both assertion and reason are true, and reason explains assertion
B. Both are true, but reason doesn't explain assertion
C. Assertion is true, reason is false
questions.explanation: The Equator is indeed the longest parallel, and it does divide the hemispheres.

questions.text: How many continents are there?
questions.explanation: The seven continents are Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Europe, North America, Oceania, and South America.

questions.text: The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean on Earth.
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean covers the largest area among all oceans.

questions.text: Which Indian state has the longest coastline?
questions.explanation: Gujarat has the longest coastline of approximately 1,600 km among Indian states.

questions.text: Match mountain ranges with their continents:
questions.explanation: Each mountain range is located on its respective continent.

questions.text: Which of the following are landlocked countries? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Nepal, Switzerland, Mongolia, and Chad have no coastline. Australia has extensive coastlines.

questions.text: The Great Barrier Reef is located off the coast of Australia.
questions.explanation: The Great Barrier Reef is the world's largest coral reef system in the Coral Sea near Queensland, Australia.

questions.text: Which is the smallest country in the world by land area?
questions.explanation: Vatican City is an independent city-state with an area of only about 0.44 square kilometers.

questions.text: Assertion: The Dead Sea is the lowest point on Earth's surface. Reason: It is a salt lake bordered by Jordan and Israel.
options: A. Both assertion and reason are true, and reason explains assertion
B. Both are true, but reason doesn't explain assertion
C. Assertion is true, reason is false
questions.explanation: Both statements are true, but the location doesn't explain why it's the lowest point; it's due to geological depression.

questions.text: The Grand Canyon is located in which US state?
questions.explanation: The Grand Canyon is in Arizona, carved by the Colorado River.

questions.text: Match rivers with the countries they primarily flow through:
questions.explanation: Each river is associated with the country it flows through.

questions.text: The Tropic of Cancer passes through India.
questions.explanation: The Tropic of Cancer passes through eight Indian states, dividing the country approximately in half.

questions.text: What is the capital city of Canada?
questions.explanation: Ottawa is the capital of Canada, although Toronto and Vancouver are larger cities.

questions.text: Which of these countries are located in Southeast Asia? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Thailand, Vietnam, Myanmar, and Philippines are Southeast Asian countries. Sri Lanka is in South Asia.

questions.text: Assertion: Deserts receive very little precipitation. Reason: They are defined by low annual rainfall.
options: A. Both assertion and reason are true, and reason explains assertion
B. Both are true, but reason doesn't explain assertion
C. Assertion is true, reason is false
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct; deserts are defined by their low precipitation levels.

questions.text: Which country is the largest in the world by total land area?
questions.explanation: Russia is the largest country by landmass, spanning Eastern Europe and Northern Asia.

questions.text: The Atacama Desert is the driest place on Earth.
questions.explanation: The Atacama Desert in Chile is considered the driest non-polar place on Earth.

questions.text: The imaginary line that divides the Earth into the Eastern and Western hemispheres is known as the Prime ______.
questions.explanation: The Prime Meridian is the line of 0 degrees longitude passing through Greenwich, England.

questions.text: Match famous landmarks with their countries:
questions.explanation: Each landmark is located in its corresponding country.

questions.text: Which of these are Scandinavian countries? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Norway, Sweden, and Denmark are Scandinavian countries. Finland is Nordic but not Scandinavian. Germany is in Central Europe.

questions.text: Mount Kilimanjaro is the highest peak in Africa.
questions.explanation: Mount Kilimanjaro is a dormant volcano in Tanzania and Africa's highest mountain.

questions.text: Which ocean is the smallest?
questions.explanation: The Arctic Ocean is the smallest of the five oceans.

questions.text: Assertion: Antarctica is technically a desert. Reason: It receives very little annual precipitation.
options: A. Both assertion and reason are true, and reason explains assertion
B. Both are true, but reason doesn't explain assertion
C. Assertion is true, reason is false
questions.explanation: Antarctica is the largest desert on Earth by definition, with minimal precipitation.

questions.text: The Suez Canal connects which two bodies of water?
questions.explanation: The Suez Canal connects the Mediterranean Sea to the Red Sea, enabling ships to travel between Europe and Asia.

questions.text: Match deserts with their continents:
questions.explanation: Each desert is located on its respective continent.

questions.text: Greenland is recognized as a fully independent sovereign nation.
questions.explanation: Greenland is an autonomous territory within the Kingdom of Denmark.

questions.text: Which of these are part of the Great Lakes of North America? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: The Great Lakes include Superior, Michigan, Huron, Erie, and Ontario. Baikal is in Russia and Victoria is in Africa.

questions.text: Which country has the most number of islands?
questions.explanation: Indonesia has over 17,000 islands, making it the country with the most islands in the world.

questions.text: Assertion: The Himalayan Mountains are the tallest mountain range. Reason: They contain the world's highest peaks.
options: A. Both assertion and reason are true, and reason explains assertion
B. Both are true, but reason doesn't explain assertion
C. Assertion is true, reason is false
questions.explanation: The Himalayan range contains the world's tallest peaks, making it the highest mountain range.

questions.text: A piece of land surrounded by water on three sides is called a ______.
questions.explanation: A peninsula is a landform with water on three sides and connected to a larger landmass.

questions.text: Which is the longest mountain range in the world?
questions.explanation: The Mid-Ocean Ridge, an underwater mountain range, is the longest at approximately 65,000 km.

questions.text: Match lakes with their countries:
questions.explanation: Each lake is located in or borders the countries mentioned.

questions.text: Which of these countries border the Mediterranean Sea? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Italy, Greece, Spain, and Egypt border the Mediterranean. Japan is on the Pacific Ocean.

questions.text: The Dead Sea is the lowest point on Earth's land surface.
questions.explanation: The Dead Sea, at about 1,410 feet below sea level, is the lowest point on Earth's surface.

questions.text: Which strait separates Spain and Morocco?
questions.explanation: The Strait of Gibraltar is the narrow passage between Spain and Morocco connecting the Mediterranean to the Atlantic.

questions.text: Assertion: Coral reefs are found only in tropical waters. Reason: They require warm, clear water to thrive.
options: A. Both assertion and reason are true, and reason explains assertion
B. Both are true, but reason doesn't explain assertion
C. Assertion is true, reason is false
questions.explanation: Coral reefs are primarily tropical due to their temperature requirements.

questions.text: The ______ River is the second-longest river in the world.
questions.explanation: The Amazon River is approximately 6,400 km long, making it the second-longest after the Nile.

questions.text: Match European capitals with their countries:
questions.explanation: These are major European capitals and their countries.

questions.text: The Arctic Circle is located at approximately 66.5 degrees north latitude.
questions.explanation: The Arctic Circle marks the southern boundary of the Arctic region.

questions.text: Which of these are major Asian capital cities? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Bangkok, Beijing, New Delhi, and Seoul are Asian capitals. Cairo is in Africa.

questions.text: Which is the largest desert in Asia?
questions.explanation: The Taklamakan Desert is the largest desert in Asia, located in western China.

questions.text: Assertion: The Panama Canal is one of the world's most important waterways. Reason: It connects the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans.
options: A. Both assertion and reason are true, and reason explains assertion
B. Both are true, but reason doesn't explain assertion
C. Assertion is true, reason is false
questions.explanation: The Panama Canal's importance comes from its connection of the two oceans.

questions.text: The ______ is the highest mountain in North America.
questions.explanation: Mount Denali (formerly Mount McKinley) in Alaska is the highest peak in North America.

questions.text: Match African countries with their capitals:
questions.explanation: These are major African countries and their capital cities.

questions.text: The Caspian Sea is the largest inland body of water.
questions.explanation: The Caspian Sea is the world's largest landlocked body of water by surface area.

questions.text: Which of these are major island groups in the Pacific Ocean? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Polynesia, Melanesia, Micronesia, and Galápagos are Pacific island groups. Caribbean Islands are in the Atlantic.

questions.text: Which country has the most number of time zones?
questions.explanation: France has 12 time zones due to its overseas territories and dependencies.

questions.text: Assertion: The International Date Line roughly follows the 180-degree meridian. Reason: It marks where the calendar date changes as you travel east or west.
options: A. Both assertion and reason are true, and reason explains assertion
B. Both are true, but reason doesn't explain assertion
C. Assertion is true, reason is false
questions.explanation: The Date Line's purpose is to manage calendar changes for travelers crossing it.

questions.text: The ______ is the deepest point in Earth's oceans.
questions.explanation: The Mariana Trench in the western Pacific Ocean is the deepest point at approximately 11,000 meters.

questions.text: Match mountain peaks with their locations:
questions.explanation: Each mountain is located in the specified country or region.
